reviews.review_description: It’s an amazing game where you need to desperately get out a sinking ship while managing your oxygen, but the only downside is that it’s very short, only around an hours length, I would like a free roam mode where there are massive seas that you can explore be added as that would make the game more fun

reviews.review_title: One of my favourite VR games but bugs
reviews.review_description: The graphics, voice acting, everything is absolutely peak. The gameplay, the sounds, everything. My only problem is that there are game-breaking bugs. Once, I turned the game on and it flung me ages away from the map and I was just in the void. Another time the water randomly vanished and I had to play like it was gorilla tag, and the bodies were just floating in mid air. The gameplay is amazing but cut kinda short even with this extended cut and I'd love to see more content. Overall the game is amazing and I love it but I wish the bugs would be removed and it would be a bit longer

I don't understand how so many ppl don't feel this game is an INTENSE arm workout?? why is this even in the game regular game catgory. I'm in fairly decent shape & I couldn't push thru the water longer than 5 mins.
I've always enjoyed underwater games. Doing it in vr is a whole new level. The story is a bit short, but other than that, 10/10.
Good gameplay and looking but extremely short
It is short, and the story is kind of boring, but it does give a good introduction to how much exercise can be done just by gaming and not actually buying an exercise app.

Escaping a sinking ship in VR is a cool idea, and the game executes the experience very well. You swim by holding down the triggers and swinging both your arms in the opposite direction of where you want to travel. This is much like how you would push water around to swim in real life, and it's fairly intuitive. Holding one trigger at a time allows you to push water away in a single direction, which turns you. You also have the ability to grab onto surfaces, as well as items, oxygen tanks, and doors. There is an oxygen level on your right wrist, as well as your current objective. You need to find items in the environment to help you progress, while monitoring your oxygen level. You can refill your oxygen by finding pockets of air, or emergency tanks posted on the walls of the ship. 

The main issues with the game are the story and the length. The story is very minimal, and serves only to explain why the ship is sinking. There is also another mystery that I won't spoil here, but it never gets fully resolved, and you never get answers. Due to this, it feels like there should be more when the credits roll, and it ultimately feels kind of unsatisfying. For something titled the "directors cut", it gives the impression that there is a lot of content, but there isn't. You can complete the game in about an hour and a half. I would not get it at the full $15 price tag. Wait for a steep sale. It works well and is a neat experience, but I wouldn't say it has any replay value.
Thoroughly enjoyed this game and found it quite inspiring. You’ll get one more star from me for a perfect score, when you upgrade it to its full potential for quest Quest 3. 😉

It was a fantastic idea, and there was lots of potential. I just did not expect to complete the game in a single game session. I kinda felt let down with this one as the story could have been lengthened with more levels.

I recently bought a quest 3 and when I saw this game a while ago I saved it so I could play it cause it looked interesting and fun. But after I bought it and started playing, I was doing the tourtorial on how to swim. Swing arms forward, turn one arm for left and right, but when I did the one to go underwater it did squares and started fritzing out. I could see the game play in the background but like it was spazing in squares of different color. I uninstalled and reinstalled the game, that didn't work. I completely shut off the game and the headset then powered it back on and it happened at the start of the game powering up. It doesn't happen to any of my other games but this one. I was really looking forward to playing the game. Could you please help me with some answers. I kinda need them

Hi, only just downloaded app and in middle of tutorial the picture has massive interference, just weird colours and shapes covering the main image so can’t see.  Only happens with this app.  I tried turning whole thing off and on again but no luck.  Any suggestions?

Edit: so I watched reviews and it looks like I finished the game and skipped a huge part of it for somehow? The game crashes once and it looks like it made me skip a big part of it

It is a nice experience until it ends very abruptly and disappointing. Graphics are okay, movement feels good.

I bought it for 15€ and I think it is only worth half of that.

The graphics and voice acting are good. However both control options are awful.  A game that relies so heavily on doing takes in such a short time frame really needs more precise controls then this game offers.

This game is very good but only English language and much people don't understand all . Please  install german language and I hope that's comming in the next time ?
What a heap of crap
Not worth $15

Other reviews that say this game is short are true. Maybe two or three hours to complete based on your play style. Short games don’t bother me if they’re well done. The game mechanics, environments and experience of this game indeed that. Well done. I felt very immersed the entire time and enjoyed my time playing this game. 

I took a star off for story. It was a good story until the end. I feel there was a lack of conclusion or clarity. I won’t elaborate because I don’t want to ruin it for anyone. It feels like the devs wanted to do more and eventually said wrap it up and ship it. 

Overall it is worth the money. Replay value is low, but that first time experience is solid.

It's starts off as a novel experience but then the controls start to become really annoying. I keep trying to go back to this but it's one of the very few VR games where I regret the purchase. The nature of the game means that it's easy to feel motion sickness as you move around even with the relevant comfort options switched on - this is possibly because you have to move around a lot to keep going back to the oxygen canisters. I've not asked for a refund as it was part of a bundle and it may be just personal preference but, for me, I just can't see what the attraction of this game would be over the many other puzzle solving games that are easier to control.
A good game but the end is not good.  They start a good story and never end it.  I hope it will continue!  I want a conclusion.

I found the game short and was left wanting more.
However its one of the most immersive experiences I've had on the quest to date. 

I would highly recommend this title.

This game is amazing the moment you  put your headset on you will be immersed into the game fully .the graphics are good for quest and won't leave you disappointed great buy.
😎😎😎
Yes it’s a short game but so very amazing. A must play game.
Way too short.  Was expecting more.  The story leaves you handing.

I’ve really enjoyed playing this game it keeps me Entertained and is really addictive. The story keeps you engaged an wondering what’s next. 

Great story telling👊🏻.  This is the closest thing to uncharted For VR even tho it’s a free diving game lol

Very enjoyable, only feedback is that it's pretty short, and there are a few bugs where you can end up out of water, or despawning the water and you have to drag yourself around as if your legs are broken.

Short and sweet, this immersive adventure will have you holding your breath until the very end.
However; if there was one recommendation I could make to the devs it would be to remove the 3rd person cutscenes during missions and allow the player to perform that final moment just before the credits roll. It'd amount to the same thing, but it'd just feel so much better if it was something the player did themselves.
Awesome
Good game but short
Love the story an experience wish there was more to it got through it too quickly having too much fun

I can’t recommend this game enough ! It’s not too expensive so definitely value for money. If you are new to the Oculus experience then this is a good first buy. Kids can play it too. IF there were more games of a similar nature I would purchase those too.
The immersion is so so only. I can buried half of my body in ground. Not realistic enough. Overall the game is good
